Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: First, note that events 1-3 and 13-15 have similar readings: low temperature (20-21C), low pressure (1 bar), vibration 0, material flow 0, tool position 0, and low energy (0.4-0.5 kWh). These likely represent idle or standby states, possibly before and after the process.

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Events 4-6 show a significant change: temperature increases to 50-58C, pressure is 1-1.5 bar, vibration is 30-38 Hz, material flow is 5 units/s, tool position increases from 5 to 10 mm, and energy consumption rises to 2-3 kWh. This suggests an active operation. High vibration and material flow might indicate cutting or machining. Could be Cutting Metal.

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Event 7-8: Temperature drops to 40C, pressure increases to 2 bar, vibration drops to 10 Hz, material flow drops to 2 units/s, tool position is fixed at 15 mm, energy is 2 kWh. This might be a different phase, perhaps Assembling Parts where pressure is higher but vibration lower.

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Event 9: Temperature spikes to 80C, pressure remains 2 bar, vibration spikes to 50 Hz, material flow is 0, tool position is 15 mm, energy spikes to 5 kWh. This looks like a high-energy, high-vibration, high-temperature operation with no material flow. Could be Welding.

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Events 10-12: Temperature drops to 25-30C, pressure drops to 1 bar, vibration is low (1-5 Hz), material flow is 0-1 units/s, tool position is 15-20 mm, energy is 1-1.6 kWh. This seems like a cooling down or inspection phase.

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So, grouping: Group A (Events 1-3): Idle/Standby ... Group B (Events 4-6): Cutting Metal Group C (Events 7-8): Assembling Parts Group D (Event 9): Welding Group E (Events 10-12): Quality Inspection Group F (Events 13-15): Idle/Standby again.

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Usually, you cut first, then assemble, then weld, then inspect, then package. So the process order might be: Cutting -> Assembling -> Welding -> Inspection -> Packaging. Given the data, events 10-12 likely Quality Inspection as after welding, inspection is common.

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Given the data, I think the most reasonable grouping is: Events 1-3: Standby Events 4-6: Cutting Metal Events 7-8: Assembling Parts Event 9: Welding Events 10-12: Quality Inspection Events 13-15: Standby.

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Rationale for each: Events 1-3: Low temperature, no vibration, no material flow, low energy. Indicates standby. Events 4-6: Temperature rises, high vibration, material flow: consistent with cutting. Events 7-8: Higher pressure, lower vibration: assembly. Event 9: Spike in temperature, vibration, energy: welding. Events 10-12: Cooling, low energy, minor movement: quality inspection. Events 13-15: Return to standby.
